answer:
In Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation Vol. III*, Chapter 36 provides the requested sketch ("Esquisse des mœurs de quelques peuples d’Europe"), which critiques Montesquieu's climatic determinism by portraying northern European peoples (especially under cold climates) as morally inferior to those farther south, attributing differences to slavery and despotism rather than latitude.[^6]

### Peoples and Key Claims
Comte focuses on a north-to-south transect, emphasizing cold-climate peoples first before contrasting with the south.

- **Lapps** (extreme north, coldest climate): Resemble Asian savages (e.g., Kamchatkans); tributary to Russians/Danes/Swedes; too weak to be malevolent but extremely lazy (work only under dire necessity), voracious (eat half a reindeer in one sitting), treat women as commodities (sold by fathers, loaned by husbands), share family beds of animal skins (lacking decency/modesty).[^1]

- **Russians** (cold climate): Inferior to southern/temperate peoples; masters and slaves alike show cruelty (in victory: massacres/tortures of vanquished, e.g., flaying Poles alive; sovereigns as executioners), servility (e.g., princes licking Tatar milk spills; senators yoking to carriages), perfidy (betraying treaties/prisoners), property insecurity (denunciations for gain, Jewish massacres), female degradation (secluded like Asians, murdered with impunity, leading to rare vices), corrupt justice (bastinado/torture for confessions), ignorance/pride. Recent "civilization" is superficial; even post-Peter the Great, a mix of barbarism and pretense.[^2]

- **Poles** (northern, not hot): Dual society of servile masses (poor/mute laborers, more liberty-valuing than Russians: revolt during wars, cruel vengeance) and domineering nobles (proud/elevated but avaricious/corrupt, bought by kings/Russia). Peasants' ferocity/stupidity reflects harsh mastership, not climate.[^3]

- **Germans** (northern/temperate): Brief; some (e.g., Rhine valley) more moral/civilized than southerners due to warmer microclimates/elevation effects; parts enjoy more liberty than Spain/Italy/Turkey.[^4]

- **Southerners (Spaniards, Italians, Turks)** (hot/temperate): Less political liberty than France/Germany but *more* (Spain/Italy) or *equal* (Turkey) to Russia; courageous when motivated (Spaniards fight vigorously for true interests; Italians held off double-sized Russians in 1812 despite alien climate/terrain). Any enslavement stems from ideas/prejudices/circumstances, *not* physical weakness/hot climates. Northern barbarism/slavery weighs on the south.[^5]

Comte concludes that cold-climate moral superiority is a myth; vices cluster where slavery reigns, regardless of latitude.[^6]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation Vol. III*, [p-68d4cb](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/36-esquisse-des-moeurs-de-quelques-peuples-d-europe-p/translation/#p-68d4cb).

[^2]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation Vol. III*, [p-6f6ce2](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/36-esquisse-des-moeurs-de-quelques-peuples-d-europe-p/translation/#p-6f6ce2); [p-a589b4](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/36-esquisse-des-moeurs-de-quelques-peuples-d-europe-p/translation/#p-a589b4); [p-c26f0a](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/36-esquisse-des-moeurs-de-quelques-peuples-d-europe-p/translation/#p-c26f0a); [p-46da53](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/36-esquisse-des-moeurs-de-quelques-peuples-d-europe-p/translation/#p-46da53).

[^3]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation Vol. III*, [p-365026](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/36-esquisse-des-moeurs-de-quelques-peuples-d-europe-p/translation/#p-365026).

[^4]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation Vol. III*, [p-1f044f](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/36-esquisse-des-moeurs-de-quelques-peuples-d-europe-p/translation/#p-1f044f).

[^5]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation Vol. III*, [p-1564ed](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/36-esquisse-des-moeurs-de-quelques-peuples-d-europe-p/translation/#p-1564ed).

[^6]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation Vol. III*, [Chapter 36](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/36-esquisse-des-moeurs-de-quelques-peuples-d-europe-p/translation/).
